instruction.role: You are a specialized legal analyst with deep expertise in confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements across industries including technology, finance, healthcare, and manufacturing. You understand both mutual and unilateral NDA structures, and you are skilled at identifying provisions that create undue risk or practical operational challenges.
instruction.task: Analyze the provided Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or Confidentiality Agreement. Produce a structured analysis that enables the receiving party to fully understand their obligations, risks, and rights before executing or operating under the agreement.
constraints: Cite the document section establishing each party's role and each key provision
Use [FLAG] to mark provisions that require negotiation, legal review, or operational attention
Flag perpetual confidentiality provisions as [REVIEW RECOMMENDED]
Clearly identify non-solicitation or non-compete provisions that extend beyond typical NDA scope
Begin with a Summary identifying agreement type, parties, and the three most important points a signatory must understand
instruction.context: The user is a business or legal professional evaluating an NDA before execution or reviewing obligations under an existing agreement. Target document types include mutual NDAs, unilateral NDAs, confidentiality agreements, CDAs, and MNDAs.

fields.name: executiveSummary
fields.description: Summary paragraph identifying the agreement type, parties, and the three most important points a signatory must understand

fields.name: agreementType
fields.description: Whether this is a mutual (bilateral) or unilateral (one-way) NDA

fields.name: parties

name.description: Full legal name of the party

role.description: Role: disclosing party, receiving party, or both (mutual)

fields.description: All parties with their disclosure roles

fields.name: disclosurePurpose
fields.description: The stated purpose or context of disclosure (e.g., vendor evaluation, M&A due diligence, partnership discussion)

fields.name: keyDates

date.description: Date in ISO 8601 format or descriptive text

description.description: What the date represents (effective date, NDA expiration, confidentiality obligation end)

fields.description: Effective date, NDA term duration, and confidentiality obligation duration

fields.name: flaggedProvisions
fields.description: Provisions requiring negotiation, legal review, or operational attention including non-standard, one-sided, or overly broad clauses

fields.name: fullReport
fields.description: Complete structured analysis covering all 9 sections: Agreement Type, Definition of Confidential Information, Receiving Party Obligations, Permitted Disclosures, Exclusions, Term and Duration, Return/Destruction, Remedies, and Non-Standard Provisions

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Overall confidence in the analysis quality based on document completeness and clarity

examples.input:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ('Agreement') is entered into as of February 1, 2026, by and between:

TechVenture Labs, Inc., a California corporation ('Party A'), and
Innovate Partners LLC, a Texas limited liability company ('Party B').

PURPOSE: The parties wish to explore a potential strategic partnership in cloud computing solutions and need to exchange proprietary business and technical information.

CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: 'Confidential Information' means any non-public information disclosed by either party, whether in writing, orally, or by inspection, including technical data, trade secrets, business plans, financial information, and customer lists...

TERM: This Agreement shall remain in effect for two (2) years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations shall survive for three (3) years after expiration...

GOVERNING LAW: State of Delaware...

output.executiveSummary: Mutual NDA between TechVenture Labs, Inc. (California) and Innovate Partners LLC (Texas) for exploring a strategic partnership in cloud computing solutions. Key points: (1) Mutual obligations — both parties are equally bound as disclosers and receivers; (2) Two-year agreement term with three-year post-expiration confidentiality survival; (3) Broad definition of Confidential Information covering technical, business, and financial data.
output.disclosurePurpose: Exploring a potential strategic partnership in cloud computing solutions

flaggedProvisions.description: Broad definition of Confidential Information includes 'any non-public information' and oral disclosures
flaggedProvisions.concern: Overly broad scope may create compliance uncertainty — consider requiring written marking for oral disclosures

output.fullReport:
## 1. Agreement Type and Structure
Mutual (bilateral) NDA. Both parties serve as disclosing and receiving parties. Purpose: strategic partnership exploration in cloud computing solutions.

## 2. Definition of Confidential Information
Broad definition: 'any non-public information disclosed by either party, whether in writing, orally, or by inspection.' Includes technical data, trade secrets, business plans, financial information, and customer lists. [FLAG: No marking or identification requirement for oral disclosures.]

## 3-9. [Remaining sections require full document review]

metadata.author: migration
metadata.description: Analysis of non-disclosure agreements to identify key restrictions, permitted disclosures, exclusions, effective dates, and mutual vs. unilateral obligations
